purpose: Defines the required structure and validation rules for a Governance Mutation Authorization (GMA). A GMA must be present, valid, and hash-matched for any PR that mutates governance primitives (governance_mutation or workflow_mutation classes).
canonical_invariant: If no valid object exists -> nothing happens.
closure_condition: Runtime governance changes require governed legitimacy approval. V1: local deterministic containment (owner dispatch + governed_files_hash binding). Post-V1: canonical /session -> /continuity -> /authority -> /compile lifecycle execution.
governed_files_hash_input: sorted list of 'path:sha256(file_content)' entries joined by newline, then sha256 of the result
governed_files_hash_scope: all governed files changed in the PR (governance/**, .github/workflows/**, src/**, schema.sql, migrations/**, wrangler.toml) — excludes governance/authorizations/**
expiry_enforcement: GMA is rejected if expires_at is in the past at merge check time
mutation_class_coverage: GMA must declare all governance_mutation and workflow_mutation classes detected in the PR
exemption_rationale: Two classes of paths are exempt from governance_mutation classification: (1) GMA artifacts at governance/authorizations/ are authorization evidence, not governance primitives — self-referentially exempt to break bootstrap circularity. (2) merge_proof_registry.jsonl is an append-only proof evidence surface that records merge events after the fact; it does not define governance rules, authority, policy, or validation logic. Evidence persistence != governance self-mutation.
enforcement_rule: governance_mutation or workflow_mutation detected AND no GMA entry in governance/authorizations/gma_registry.jsonl (or, as a transitional fallback, governance/authorizations/GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json) matching this PR's governed diff AND no valid Standing Authority covering the governed diff (GAP-005 Phase 2 derivation) -> MERGE_LEGITIMACY_NULL

admission_tiers.note: merge-governance-check evaluates authorization in tiers. Tiers 1-2 are explicit per-PR GMAs (one mutation each). Tier 3 derives a scoped authorization from a bounded, owner-issued Standing Authority (a CLASS of mutations) — see governance/authorizations/STANDING_AUTHORITY_SPEC.json. Compressed rule: manual GMA governs one mutation; Standing Authority governs a bounded class of mutations; both must end in proof.
admission_tiers.tier_1: Explicit per-PR GMA in gma_registry.jsonl, keyed by (branch, governed_files_hash).
admission_tiers.tier_2: Legacy singleton GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json, keyed by governed_files_hash (transitional fallback).
admission_tiers.tier_3: Standing Authority derivation via runtime/standing-authority.mjs#selectStandingAuthority against governance/authorizations/standing_authority_registry.jsonl, bounded by branch_pattern + mutation_classes + path_globs + TTL (hard) + max_merges (async proof-ledger budget). Each derived merge stamps standing_authority_id into its merge proof.

registry_model.format: JSON Lines — one immutable GMA object per line, append-only
registry_model.rationale: The prior model stored a single mutable GMA file overwritten by every governance-mutation PR, causing concurrent governance PRs to collide on the same artifact and forcing rebase/reissuance loops (observed: PR #1982 closed as redundant after colliding with main). Append-only lineage lets each PR issue its own immutable GMA entry without contending for a shared file.
registry_model.selection_rule: merge-governance-check selects the gma_registry.jsonl entry where entry.branch === this PR's head ref AND entry.governed_files_hash === this PR's computed governed_files_hash AND entry.status === 'GMA_VALID' AND entry.expires_at is in the future AND entry.authority_lineage_bound === true AND entry.mutation_classes covers all governance_mutation/workflow_mutation classes detected in the PR. If multiple entries match, the most recently created one is used.
registry_model.branch_binding_rationale: Binding to the requesting branch (not just content hash) prevents a GMA issued for one branch's diff from authorizing an identical-hash mutation proposed on a different branch (replay safety). head_sha is intentionally NOT part of the selection key because it changes on every rebase/push; binding to branch keeps a previously issued GMA valid across rebases as long as the governed diff is unchanged.
registry_model.append_only_enforcement: merge-governance-check includes a dedicated step ('Enforce append-only GMA registry') that, when gma_registry.jsonl is among the PR's changed files, asserts the base-branch version of the file is a byte-exact prefix of the head-branch version — i.e. existing entries may never be modified or removed, only new entries appended. This is required because governance/authorizations/** is exempt from normal governed-file classification and would otherwise be invisible to the governance check.

merge_union_driver.effect: On a local/CLI 3-way merge or rebase, conflicting appended lines from both sides are kept (union) instead of raising a conflict — eliminating mechanical line conflicts between concurrent registry-appending branches.
merge_union_driver.limitation: merge=union is honored by git's local merge/rebase machinery only. It does NOT apply to GitHub's server-side 'Merge'/'Rebase and merge' buttons, which do not invoke local merge drivers. The semantic fix against contention is the lookup-by-(branch, governed_files_hash) selection rule above, which works regardless of merge venue; merge=union is a supplementary mechanical aid for local/CLI workflows.

phases.phase_1: Issuance workflow dual-writes: appends a GMA entry to gma_registry.jsonl AND continues writing the legacy singleton GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json.
phases.phase_2: Validation is registry-first: gma_registry.jsonl is checked via the (branch, governed_files_hash) selection rule; if no match is found, the legacy singleton is checked as a fallback (matched by governed_files_hash alone, unbound to branch).
phases.phase_3: Observe several real governance/workflow-mutation PRs admitted via the registry path with no singleton-fallback usage.
phases.phase_4: Deprecate the singleton: stop dual-writing GOVERNANCE_MUTATION_AUTHORIZATION.json in the issuance workflow.
phases.phase_5: Remove the singleton file and its fallback branch from merge-governance-check entirely.

migration.note: This spec currently reflects phases 1-2. Phases 3-5 are explicit follow-ups and must not be performed prematurely — the singleton fallback is a deliberate de-risking measure during cutover.

v1_issuance_model.note: V1 eliminates external Worker availability dependency. governed_files_hash provides content binding. Owner workflow_dispatch provides explicit authorization. Canonical runtime lifecycle execution is SEQUENCED for post-V1 once WORKER_URL/API_KEY authorization is restored.

external_runtime_issuance.blocker: MINDSHIFT_WORKER_URL / MINDSHIFT_API_KEY authorization failure (HTTP 403 on PREFLIGHT)
external_runtime_issuance.note: When external runtime is available, governance-mutation-authorization.yml should be extended to optionally call the canonical lifecycle and produce decision_id/continuity_id from /session → /continuity → /authority → /compile.
